original_reason: Hand & Wrist Anatomy Hub missing outbound part_of (0/1).
reason: Root/regional anatomy hub should not require an outbound part_of edge to satisfy a child-to-parent anatomy rule; creating a self-edge or arbitrary parent would be misleading.
Scapholunate ligament hierarchy is not essential to the CTS vertical. Deferral does not alter CTS inference because this ligament is not used in CTS clinical claims, decisions, or relationships.
Lunotriquetral ligament hierarchy is not essential to the CTS vertical. Deferral does not create misleading CTS inference.
TFCC hierarchy is not essential to the CTS vertical. Deferral does not create misleading CTS inference.
Thumb UCL hierarchy is not essential to the CTS vertical. Deferral does not create misleading CTS inference.
Volar plate hierarchy is not essential to the CTS vertical. Deferral does not create misleading CTS inference.
Collateral ligament hierarchy is not essential to the CTS vertical. Deferral does not create misleading CTS inference.
Carpal tunnel is central CTS anatomy and can safely be attached to the hand/wrist anatomy hub.
Guyon canal hierarchy is not essential to the CTS vertical. It is relevant to ulnar neuropathy/Guyon canal content, not core CTS staging.

original_reason: Carpal Tunnel Syndrome neighborhood has 0/1 classification_system entities.

resolution.scope_note: Clinical diagnostic scoring/tool concept for CTS probability; not a severity grade system.

reason: Evidence packet cites AAOS use of CTS-6 in place of routine ultrasound or NCV/EMG for typical CTS evaluation.

original_reason: Carpal Tunnel Syndrome neighborhood has 0/1 imaging_finding entities.
reason: Routine imaging is not central to typical CTS diagnosis/follow-up; creating an imaging finding solely to satisfy a generic rule would create misleading inference.

original_reason: Carpal Tunnel Syndrome missing outbound has_classification (0/1).
reason: Resolved by the CTS-6 clinical diagnostic tool relationship in the normalized input.

original_reason: Carpal Tunnel Syndrome missing outbound has_grade (0/1).
reason: No named CTS severity grade system was supplied or approved. The evidence packet explicitly says not to invent grades or universal EDX thresholds.

original_reason: Carpal Tunnel Syndrome missing outbound has_imaging_finding (0/1).
reason: For typical CTS, a required imaging-finding edge would contradict the evidence packet's warning against routine imaging as the diagnostic/follow-up pathway.
